mission.title: Install and Configure Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)
mission.description: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the process of running Kubernetes on AWS without needing to install and operate your own control plane or nodes. It's ideal for users looking for a scalable and secure Kubernetes solution integrated with AWS services.

steps.title: Create an EKS Cluster
steps.description:
Use the AWS CLI to create an EKS cluster. Replace `<your-cluster-name>` and `<your-region>` with your desired values. Ensure you have the AWS CLI configured with the necessary permissions.

```bash
aws eks create-cluster --name <your-cluster-name> --region <your-region> --kubernetes-version 1.31 --role-arn arn:aws:iam::<your-account-id>:role/EKS-ClusterRole --resources-vpc-config subnetIds=<subnet-1,subnet-2>,securityGroupIds=<sg-id>
```

Make sure to replace the placeholders with your actual values.

steps.title: Configure kubectl
steps.description:
After the cluster is created, configure kubectl to connect to your new EKS cluster:

```bash
aws eks update-kubeconfig --name <your-cluster-name> --region <your-region>
```

Verify the connection with:
```bash
kubectl cluster-info
```

steps.title: Install the EKS Add-ons
steps.description:
Install necessary add-ons like CoreDNS and VPC CNI:

```bash
aws eks create-addon --cluster-name <your-cluster-name> --addon-name coredns --service-account-role-arn arn:aws:iam::<your-account-id>:role/EKS-AddonRole
aws eks create-addon --cluster-name <your-cluster-name> --addon-name vpc-cni
```

steps.title: Verify Cluster and Node Status
steps.description:
Check the status of your nodes and ensure they are ready:

```bash
kubectl get nodes
```

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up Cluster Autoscaler for your EKS cluster:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/aws/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Delete the EKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
To uninstall EKS, delete the cluster and associated resources:

```bash
aws eks delete-cluster --name <your-cluster-name> --region <your-region>
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade EKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
To upgrade your EKS cluster to a newer version, first check the current version and then initiate the upgrade:

```bash
aws eks describe-cluster --name <your-cluster-name> --query 'cluster.version'
aws eks update-cluster-version --name <your-cluster-name> --kubernetes-version 1.31 --region <your-region>
```

Backup your data before upgrading. Use:
```bash
kubectl get all --all-namespaces -o yaml > backup.yaml
```

Rollback if necessary by reverting to the previous version using:
```bash
aws eks update-cluster-version --name <your-cluster-name> --kubernetes-version <previous-version> --region <your-region>
```

troubleshooting.title: Cluster Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access your cluster, check your kubeconfig and IAM permissions:
```bash
aws eks update-kubeconfig --name <your-cluster-name> --region <your-region>
``` Ensure your IAM role has the necessary permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Node Not Ready
troubleshooting.description:
If nodes are not in 'Ready' state, check the events:
```bash
kubectl describe nodes <node-name>
``` Look for any errors related to networking or IAM roles.

troubleshooting.title: Add-on Installation Failure
troubleshooting.description:
If an add-on fails to install, check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -n kube-system <addon-pod-name>
``` Ensure that the add-on version is compatible with Kubernetes 1.31.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for Kubernetes 1.31, improved IAM role configurations.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older IAM roles for EKS cluster management.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of Amazon EKS should result in a fully functional Kubernetes cluster with nodes in a 'Ready' state, accessible via kubectl, and configured with necessary add-ons for operational efficiency.
codeSnippets:
aws eks create-addon --cluster-name <your-cluster-name> --addon-name coredns --service-account-role-arn arn:aws:iam::<your-account-id>:role/EKS-AddonRole
aws eks create-addon --cluster-name <your-cluster-name> --addon-name vpc-cni

prerequisites.description: Ensure AWS CLI is configured with appropriate permissions to create and manage EKS resources.
